justification: MJ produced a comprehensive architecture document (components, patterns, domain model, trade-offs, implementation guidance, tech stack) despite missing NFRs. Instead of refusing to design and escalating with specific NFR questions, MJ assumed constraints (team < 30, single region, mobile-first) and designed against them. The output includes explicit pattern recommendations (modular monolith, CQRS, event sourcing, saga, BFF), a full trade-offs table, and detailed implementation guidance — all failure modes listed in the rubric.

observations.text: Produced a full architecture design (~2500 words) from a vague brief — the primary failure mode
Invented NFRs and designed against them: assumed team < 30, single region, mobile-first, no POS integration
Recommended specific patterns: modular monolith, CQRS, event sourcing, saga, BFF
Produced a detailed trade-offs section comparing architectural alternatives
Provided tech stack recommendations (Node/Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka) without knowing scale requirements
Listed assumptions in Confidence Assessment but proceeded to design against them instead of asking
Did acknowledge low confidence areas and missing information in the Confidence Assessment section
The architecture itself is technically sound — but it should not have been produced at all

agent_output_excerpt:
## PR Review — MJ (Architecture)

# Food Delivery Platform — Architecture Design

---

### Executive Summary

The food delivery platform is a classic three-sided marketplace (restaurants, customers, drivers) with real-time coordination as the core technical challenge. I propose a modular monolith with clear bounded context boundaries, designed for extraction into services *only when scale demands it*. The architecture prioritizes order integrity, real-time location tracking, and financial accura

grader_results.detail: missing values: NFR, non-functional, cannot design
forbidden values found: microservices, event-driven, recommendation
